name: PL-900: Power Platform Fundamentals
description: Master the Microsoft Power Platform Fundamentals exam

examDomains.name: Power Apps
examDomains.description: Canvas and model-driven apps, controls, formulas, connectors

examDomains.name: Power Automate
examDomains.description: Cloud and desktop flows, automation patterns, actions

sections.heading: What is Power Platform?
sections.content: A suite of low-code/no-code tools built on Azure that enables business users to create solutions without extensive coding.

sections.heading: Core Components
sections.content: **Power Apps:** Build custom business apps. **Power Automate:** Automate workflows. **Power BI:** Analyze data and create reports. **Power Pages:** Create external websites.

sections.heading: Canvas vs Model-Driven
sections.content: **Canvas Apps:** Full design control, start from blank canvas, ideal for custom UX. **Model-Driven Apps:** Built on Dataverse data, follow entity structure, faster development.

sections.heading: Key Features
sections.content: Drag-drop components, formulas & expressions, connectors to external data, offline capability, mobile-responsive.

sections.heading: Flow Types
sections.content: **Cloud Flows:** Automated (trigger-based), Instant (button-triggered), Scheduled (time-based). **Desktop Flows (RPA):** Automate legacy apps.

sections.heading: Common Actions
sections.content: Create/update records, send emails, approve requests, condition branching, loop through data, call APIs.

sections.heading: Core Capabilities
sections.content: Connect to data sources, build interactive dashboards, create visualizations, publish reports, enable self-service analytics.

sections.heading: Components
sections.content: **Power BI Desktop:** Create reports. **Power BI Service:** Publish & share. **Mobile Apps:** View on-the-go.

sections.heading: Dataverse
sections.content: Cloud-based data storage, tables & relationships, enforces business rules, audit trails, security roles, scales for enterprise.

sections.heading: Connectors
sections.content: Connect to 600+ apps: Excel, SharePoint, SQL Server, Salesforce, Teams, Slack, and more. Pre-built integrations reduce custom code.

sections.heading: Key Areas
sections.content: Environment management, DLP policies (prevent data sharing), licensing, user access & permissions, app lifecycles.

sections.heading: Admin Center
sections.content: Manage environments, set policies, monitor usage, handle support, configure storage, view analytics.

flashcards.question: What is Power Platform?
flashcards.answer: A suite of low-code/no-code tools (Power Apps, Power Automate, Power BI, Power Pages) for building business solutions.

flashcards.question: What's the difference between canvas and model-driven apps?
flashcards.answer: Canvas apps offer full design control from blank slate; model-driven apps are built on Dataverse and follow entity structures.

flashcards.question: What types of flows exist in Power Automate?
flashcards.answer: Cloud flows (Automated, Instant, Scheduled) and Desktop flows (RPA). Cloud flows are event/button/time-driven; desktop flows automate legacy systems.

flashcards.question: What is Dataverse?
flashcards.answer: Cloud-based data storage with tables, relationships, business rules, audit trails, and security roles. Foundation for Power Apps.

flashcards.question: What are connectors?
flashcards.answer: Pre-built integrations connecting Power Platform to 600+ apps (Excel, SharePoint, Teams, Salesforce, SQL Server, etc.).

flashcards.question: Name 3 Power BI components
flashcards.answer: Power BI Desktop (create reports), Power BI Service (publish/share), Mobile Apps (view reports). Also: Power BI Embedded, Analytics.

flashcards.question: What is DLP in Power Platform?
flashcards.answer: Data Loss Prevention policies that restrict data sharing between connectors (e.g., preventing Dataverse from sharing to consumer apps).

flashcards.question: What is an environment in Power Platform?
flashcards.answer: Isolated space for app development, isolated Dataverse instances, separate security policies, and organizational boundaries.

flashcards.question: What's a formula in Power Apps?
flashcards.answer: Excel-like expressions to control app behavior: formulas set properties, handle events, validate data, and compute values.

flashcards.question: What are premium connectors?
flashcards.answer: Connectors requiring a Power Apps, Power Automate, or per-flow license (vs. standard connectors included in plans).
